messages.content: What is DNA?
DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) is the molecule that carries the genetic instructions for the development, functioning, and reproduction of all known living organisms.
What is a cell?
The basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Every living thing is made of one or more cells. Cells contain genetic material and carry out life processes.
What is DNA?
DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) is the molecule that carries the genetic instructions for the development, functioning, and reproduction of all known living organisms.
What is a virus?
A microscopic infectious agent made of genetic material (DNA or RNA) inside a protein coat. Viruses can only replicate inside a living host cell.
How many bones are in the adult human body?
206 bones.
What does the brain do?
The brain controls all bodily functions, processes sensory information, regulates emotions, stores memories, and enables thinking, language, and consciousness.
What is blood made of?
Blood has four components: red blood cells (carry oxygen), white blood cells (fight infection), platelets (help clotting), and plasma (liquid that carries everything).
What does the brain do?
The brain controls all bodily functions, processes sensory information, regulates emotions, stores memories, and enables thinking, language, and consciousness.
What is the immune system?
The body's defense system against disease. It recognizes and destroys pathogens (bacteria, viruses, parasites) and abnormal cells like cancer cells.

What is metabolism?
The set of chemical processes that occur in living organisms to maintain life — converting food to energy, building and breaking down molecules, and eliminating waste.
What is evolution?

The process by which species change over time through natural selection — individuals with traits better suited to their environment are more likely to survive and reproduce, passing those traits to offspring.
What does the brain do?

What is the difference between bacteria and viruses?

Bacteria are single-celled living organisms that can reproduce on their own. Viruses are not cells — they are genetic material in a protein shell that can only reproduce by infecting a host cell. Antibiotics kill bacteria but don't work on viruses.

A microscopic infectious agent made of genetic material (DNA or RNA) inside a protein coat. Viruses can only replicate inside a living host cell.
How does photosynthesis work?
Plants use chlorophyll to absorb sunlight, then combine carbon dioxide from the air with water from the soil to produce glucose (food) and oxygen. The equation: CO₂ + H₂O + sunlight → glucose + O₂.
What is blood made of?
Blood has four components: red blood cells (carry oxygen), white blood cells (fight infection), platelets (help clotting), and plasma (liquid that carries everything).
What is a cell?
The basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Every living thing is made of one or more cells. Cells contain genetic material and carry out life processes.
What is the difference between bacteria and viruses?

Bacteria are single-celled living organisms that can reproduce on their own. Viruses are not cells — they are genetic material in a protein shell that can only reproduce by infecting a host cell. Antibiotics kill bacteria but don't work on viruses.
What is evolution?

The process by which species change over time through natural selection — individuals with traits better suited to their environment are more likely to survive and reproduce, passing those traits to offspring.

How does photosynthesis work?
Plants use chlorophyll to absorb sunlight, then combine carbon dioxide from the air with water from the soil to produce glucose (food) and oxygen. The equation: CO₂ + H₂O + sunlight → glucose + O₂.
What does the heart do?

What is a virus?
A microscopic infectious agent made of genetic material (DNA or RNA) inside a protein coat. Viruses can only replicate inside a living host cell.
How does the digestive system work?
Food enters through the mouth, is broken down by stomach acids and enzymes, nutrients are absorbed in the small intestine, and waste passes through the large intestine and exits as stool.
What is evolution?

The process by which species change over time through natural selection — individuals with traits better suited to their environment are more likely to survive and reproduce, passing those traits to offspring.
What is a virus?
A microscopic infectious agent made of genetic material (DNA or RNA) inside a protein coat. Viruses can only replicate inside a living host cell.
What is metabolism?
The set of chemical processes that occur in living organisms to maintain life — converting food to energy, building and breaking down molecules, and eliminating waste.
What is the immune system?
The body's defense system against disease. It recognizes and destroys pathogens (bacteria, viruses, parasites) and abnormal cells like cancer cells.
What is blood made of?
Blood has four components: red blood cells (carry oxygen), white blood cells (fight infection), platelets (help clotting), and plasma (liquid that carries everything).
What is a gene?
A sequence of DNA that encodes the instructions for building a specific protein. Genes are the units of heredity passed from parents to offspring.
What is a cell?
The basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Every living thing is made of one or more cells. Cells contain genetic material and carry out life processes.
